operating_state: Net sales increased 9% to $57.4 billion driven by 7% comparable sales growth and 24 net new warehouses opened since prior year. Performance was broadly positive across all regions with mid-to-high single digit growth. E-commerce sales accelerated 21% while membership fees grew 7.6%, with increased shopping frequency and slightly higher average ticket driving comparable sales growth.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage increased 52 basis points to 10.84%, primarily driven by the absence of prior year charter shipping discontinuation charge. SG&A expenses as a percentage of sales decreased 15 basis points to 8.96% from improved warehouse productivity and operational leverage. Operating income expanded 30.9% despite inflationary headwinds and gasoline operations pressuring margins by 5 basis points.
material_events: The company paid a special dividend of $15.00 per share totaling $6.7 billion in January 2024 and increased quarterly dividend to $1.16 per share. Share repurchases totaled $484 million with $3.1 billion remaining under authorized program through January 2027. Japan subsidiary issued $500 million in Guaranteed Senior Notes with maturities from 2033 to 2043.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in $117 million negative impact year-to-date compared to $149 million positive impact in prior year. Higher gasoline prices positively impacted net sales by $149 million in Q3 2024. Government tariff actions, particularly between China and the United States, continue to affect merchandise costs with exposure dependent on goods type, rates imposed, and timing.
forward_outlook: Management plans to open 14 additional warehouses in remainder of fiscal 2024 and expects total capital expenditures of approximately $4.3 to $4.5 billion for the full year. The company maintains strategy of investing in merchandise pricing to preserve pricing authority with members, which may pressure near-term gross margins. Management believes current cash position and operating cash flows will be sufficient to meet liquidity and capital requirements.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple employment-related class action lawsuits pending regarding wage and hour claims with immaterial accruals recorded. Opioid litigation consolidated in federal MDL with claims from various municipalities remains ongoing. Rising employee costs, particularly healthcare and wages, continue to pressure margins given the company's low-margin operating model where modest cost changes can substantially impact net income.
